mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean direction during payroll growth periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >50% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, creating positive short-term market momentum and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 5-day returns in >65% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
